title: I Promessi Sposi (Quarantana, 1840): a facsimile-aligned TEI edition
description:
A TEI encoding of Alessandro Manzoni's I promessi sposi whose reading text is aligned, line by line, to a digital facsimile of the Quarantana (the illustrated 1840 Milan edition Manzoni himself supervised). The reading text follows Teresa Poggi Salani's critical edition (2013), whose copy-text is the 1840 Quarantana edition; the facsimile layer reproduces a physical copy of that same edition owned by Paola Italia, digitized by FICLIT/AdLab and served over IIIF by the FICLIT Digital Library, Università di Bologna.

The dataset is a curated release: initial line regions were generated by automated layout analysis and then manually reviewed page by page in eScriptorium. It comprises 742 page surfaces, 20,579 aligned line zones (complete linkage coverage within the encoded introduction and 38 chapters), 438 illustrations linked to TEI figure elements, a 505-item illustration catalogue with precomputed IIIF region URLs, a TEI ODD with embedded Schematron project rules, and an assembled single-file TEI edition. Page images are not redistributed: only coordinates and IIIF URLs are included, and the images remain served from the FICLIT endpoint. The text is a critical reading text aligned to — not transcribed from — the printed witness; a dated validation report documents the integrity checks performed at release.

contributors.name: FICLIT Digital Library, Università di Bologna

notes: The TEI text encoding is from the LeggoManzoni project (quaranta commenti alla quarantana), part of the Manzoni Online initiative (https://www.alessandromanzoni.org). The digitized volume is the personal copy of Paola Italia; it was digitized by FICLIT/AdLab, and the digital copy is held and served by the FICLIT Digital Library, Università di Bologna. Images are not redistributed. Component-level rights are itemized in RIGHTS.md; contributor roles in CONTRIBUTORS.md.
